# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/bin/bash -e
# Script setup variables are read here for use throughout scripts, they will override defaults when run as part of a larger pipeline if required (i.e., set with `-v` flag) or inside specific jobs in CI systems like Jenkins etc that require input from user and default to local environment variable values 
AWS_ACCESS_KEY="${awsAccessKey:-default}" # Provide AWS Access Key here, if not provided by the caller. This will be overridden when run as part of larger pipeline or inside specific jobs in CI systems for security reasons (set with -e flag to fail upon error)
AWS_SECRET_KEY="${awsSecretKey:-default}" # Provide AWS Secret Key here, if not provided by the caller. This will be overridden when run as part of larger pipeline or inside specific jobs in CI systems for security reasons (set with -e flag to fail upon error)
REGION_NAME="${regionName:-"us-west-2"}" # Provide AWS Region here, if not provided by the caller. This will be overridden when run as part of larger pipeline or inside specific jobs in CI systems (default is 'us-west-2')
AWS_PROFILE="${awsProfile:-""}" 	# If you have multiple profiles then provide AWS profile name here, if not provided by the caller. This will be overridden when run as part of larger pipeline or inside specific jobs in CI systems (default is ''). It should match one from your ~/.aws/credentials file
DEPLOYMENT_BUCKET="${deploymentBucket:-"my-bucket"}" # Provide the S3 Bucket name for storing deployment artifacts here, if not provided by caller. (default is 'my-bucket') 
AWS_CLI_VERSION=$(aws --version | sed -n '2p'|awk -F ":") ; echo ${AWS_CLI_VERSION} # Print the version of AWS CLI being used for security reasons, not required but can be removed if unneeded.
export DEPLOYMENT_BUCKET 
# Authenticate to aws cli using either default profile or provided custom userdata in environment variables -v flag needed only when you want a more verbose output of the command and are sure about AWS credentials, not recommended for CI/CD pipelines.   #aws configure set region $REGION_NAME --profile my-user
# echo "Setting up authentication to aws"  (Can be removed if unnecessary)
AWS=$(which aws | sed -n '2p') ; eval ${AWS} configure set aws_access_key_id  $AWS_ACCESS_KEY; #aws access key id for IAM user or role. This will override the default value when run as part of larger pipeline, not required by CI/CD pipelines (set with -e flag to fail upon error)
eval ${AWS} configure set aws_secret_access_key  $AWS_SECRET_KEY; #aws secret key for IAM user or role. This will override the default value when run as part of larger pipeline, not required by CI/CD pipelines (set with -e flag to fail upon error)
eval ${AWS} configure set region   $REGION_NAME ; 	#Setting up AWS Region here if it's different from Default in aws configuration. This will override the default value when run as part of larger pipeline or inside specific jobs on CI/CD system (default is 'us-west-2')
eval ${AWS} configure set profile  $AWS_PROFILE ; # If you have multiple profiles then provide AWS Profile name here if not provided by caller. This will override the default value when run as part of larger pipeline or inside specific jobs on CI/CD system (default is ''). It should match one from your ~/.aws/credentials file
echo "Authenticating to Amazon S3"  # Can be removed, depends if you want a more verbose output. Unnecessary with -e flag for error handling in AWS CLI scripts as the authentication part of script can fail at any point (set by default) unless explicitly set otherwise before run from larger pipeline or inside specific jobs on CI/CD system
eval ${AWS} s3 ls # Checking if S3 Bucket is available, should return a list and print bucket name. This will override the defaults when called as part of large pipelines (set with -e flag to fail upon error) 
# echo "S3 Deployment begins" can be removed for more verbosity in larger scripts but not necessary here if you only need AWS CLI deployment operations, even then it's recommended. Unnecessary unless there are specific requirements as part of CI/CD pipelines or large scale deployments with multiple steps and fail on any error
# echo "Deployment to S3 begins"  (can be removed for more verbosity in larger scripts but not necessary here if you only need AWS CLI deployment operations, even then it's recommended. Unnecessary unless there are specific requirements as part of CI/CD pipelines or large scale deployments with multiple steps and fail on any error
# echo "Deployment done"  (can be removed for more verbosity in larger scripts but not necessary here if you only need AWS CLI deployment operations, even then it's recommended. Unnecessary unless there are specific requirements as part of CI/CD pipelines or large scale deployments with multiple steps and fail on any error
